title: Protocol: PEPFAR-focused HIV infrastructure and non-communicable disease trial capacity in Africa
body: This cross-sectional registry study will test whether PEPFAR-built HIV clinical research infrastructure is associated with broader non-communicable disease trial capacity across sub-Saharan Africa. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for HIV trials, NCD trials covering cardiovascular disease, diabetes, hypertension, cancer, and stroke, and total interventional trials across ten major PEPFAR-focus countries receiving over one hundred million dollars annually and eight non-PEPFAR comparator nations with similar income levels. The primary outcome is the PEPFAR Spillover Index, defined as NCD trials divided by HIV trials for each country, with values above one suggesting successful infrastructure transfer. Secondary metrics include per-capita HIV and NCD trial density, an HIV Dominance Score quantifying each country structural dependency on HIV research, and group-level comparisons between PEPFAR and non-PEPFAR cohorts. Query logic, disease definitions, and comparator selection will be prespecified and version controlled. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include registry dependence, ecological design, and inability to infer causation from cross-sectional comparisons.

sentences.text: This cross-sectional registry study will test whether HIV-focused research infrastructure is associated with broader non-communicable disease trial capacity in sub-Saharan Africa.
We will query ClinicalTrials.gov for HIV trials, NCD trials, and total interventional trials across ten major PEPFAR-focus countries and eight non-PEPFAR comparators with similar income levels.
The primary outcome is the PEPFAR Spillover Index, defined as NCD trials divided by HIV trials.
Secondary metrics include per-capita HIV and NCD trial density and an HIV dominance score for each country.
Query logic, disease definitions, and comparator selection will be prespecified and version controlled.
No patient-level data will be used.
Limitations include registry dependence, ecological design, and inability to infer causation from cross-sectional comparisons.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 10 PEPFAR-focus + 8 non-PEPFAR African countries
